definition: An OPM model element is a thing or a link. Things are objects and processes; links express structural or procedural relations.
example: Elements = things + links; objects may have states.

definition: An OPM thing is an object or a process. Objects exist; processes happen to objects. Each depends on the other: a process needs >=1 object to transform; transforming an object needs a process.
example: Object and Process are Things.

definition: Founder term: Entity generalizes Thing and State, i.e., {object, process, state}. ISO 19450 does not define 'entity'; it uses 'element' = thing or link.
example: State and Thing are Entities. Object and Process are Things.

definition: Model element representing a thing that does or might exist physically or informatically. Persistent: remains in its current implicit or explicit state until a process acts on it. A model object is a category pattern for operational instances.
opl_template: Object is essence and affiliation object.
example: Vehicle Occupant Group (rectangle).

definition: An object is a thing that has the potential of stable, unconditional physical or mental (informatical) existence. (OCR-restored from book definition box.)
example: Static things; changed only by processes.
source_reference: Dori 2002 sec 4.4; front matter Building Blocks

definition: Transformation of one or more objects: generation (creation), effect (state change), or consumption (elimination). Positive time duration. Never exists in isolation; always associated with objects.
example: Automatic Crash Responding (ellipse).

definition: A process is a pattern of transformation that an object undergoes. Dynamic things, recognizable by the changes they cause to objects.
example: Processing names use gerund '-ing' form.

definition: A noun denotes a process iff ALL hold: (1) time association; (2) verb association; (3) it transforms or state-maintains >=1 object. Default: nouns are objects.
example: Flight passes all three criteria: time, verb 'to fly', transforms Airplane location.

definition: A state is a possible situation or position of an object, meaningful only within the owning object. Stateless object: no states specified. Stateful: explicit permissible state set; at runtime an instance is at exactly one state or in transition between two.
opl_template: Object can be s1 or s2.
example: Museum Visitor can be inside the museum or out of the museum.

definition: Initial state: at system start or upon generation. Final: at completion or upon consumption. Default: most likely upon random inspection. 0+ initial, 0+ final, 0..1 default per object; roles combinable.
opl_template: State s of Object is initial|default|final.
example: State preliminary of Specification is initial.

definition: A value is a state of an attribute; an attribute is an object characterizing another thing. Permissible values = permissible states (enumerated or ranges). Measurement units in brackets (OPD) and inline (OPL).
opl_template: Attribute of Exhibitor can be v1, v2, and v3. / ... ranges from x to y.
example: Temperature in degrees Celsius of Engine; 75 is a value.

definition: OPM does not model process states such as started/in-process/finished; status is modelled by subprocesses (e.g., Starting, Processing, Finishing).
example: Use subprocess decomposition, not status attributes on processes.

definition: Every thing has Perseverance (static=object/dynamic=process), Essence (physical/informatical), Affiliation (systemic/environmental). Defaults: Affiliation=systemic; Essence=system Primary Essence. Physical=shaded; environmental=dashed.
opl_template: Thing is physical|informatical and systemic|environmental thing-kind.
example: Physical Environmental Object is a physical and environmental object.

definition: Graphical representation of an OPM model or part of one: objects, processes, structural and procedural links. One OPD = one context. SD (System Diagram) = root OPD with one systemic process (the function).
example: OPD process tree rooted at SD navigates the model.

definition: Subset of English representing textually exactly what the OPD represents graphically. Bimodality: each OPD has an equivalent OPL paragraph; each construct (link+endpoints) one OPL sentence. Not two models - two views of one model.
example: Annex A of ISO 19450 gives normative EBNF.

definition: Transformation = creation, consumption, or state change; only processes transform. Transformee roles: consumee (consumed), resultee (created), affectee (state-changed). Roles are per-process: one object may be enabler for one process, transformee for another.
example: A file: read-only -> instrument; edited -> affectee; deleted -> consumee; created -> resultee.

definition: Enabler: object a process needs but does not transform; present throughout performance; its disappearance immediately ends the process. Agent = human enabler; instrument = non-human enabler.
example: Moving Truck enables Moving but is unchanged.

definition: Preprocess set (consumees, affectees, enablers; possibly state-specified) determines the precondition; postprocess set (resultees, affectees, enablers) the postcondition. Both non-empty for every process. Involved set = union.
example: Consumees pre only; resultees post only; enablers and affectees in both.

definition: Event (object creation/appearance/state entry) triggers precondition evaluation of linked processes; the event is then lost; process performs iff precondition satisfied. Flow of execution control = successive ECA sequences.
example: Events and preconditions in concert control process performance.

definition: Non-condition incoming links: process waits for the source to exist (possibly at state). Condition ('c') links: if source missing, the process is skipped/bypassed. Skip overrides wait when both kinds are unsatisfied.
example: One failed condition -> process skipped, control passes on.

definition: A model fact in one OPD is true across all OPDs; no OPD may contradict another. Refinement/abstraction of a fact is not contradiction.
example: 'P yields A.' contradicts 'P consumes A.'; 'P affects A.' vs 'P changes A from s1 to s2.' is refinement.

definition: A process connects via a transforming link to >=1 object/state. At a given abstraction, an object or one of its states has exactly one role and exactly one procedural link to a given process.
example: An object cannot be both instrument and consumee of the same process in one OPD level.

definition: When abstraction makes links compete, retain the strongest: consumption event > consumption = result > consumption condition > effect event > effect > effect condition > agent event > agent > agent condition > instrument event > instrument > instrument condition.
example: Result link prevails over effect link when out-zooming.

definition: Refinement/abstraction pairs: state expression/suppression; unfolding/folding (parts, features, specializations, instances); in-zooming/out-zooming (process in-zoom orders subprocesses temporally top-down; object in-zoom orders parts spatially).
example: OPD process tree built by in-zooming from SD.

definition: Conceptual model = pattern of structure/behaviour; behaviour occurs only when operational instances of things exist. Distinguish refinee instance (classification-instantiation) from operational instance (runtime occurrence).
example: A link between things implies no behaviour until operational instances exist.

definition: Function = process providing functional value to a beneficiary; same function can be achieved by different behaviours/designs (bridge vs ferry for River Crossing). Begin modelling by naming the system function as primary process (SD).
example: River Crossing identical for bridge and ferry; structure/behaviour differ.

definition: Static, time-independent associations persisting for some interval; never contingent on time-dependent conditions. Connect like-kind things (object-object or process-process) EXCEPT exhibition-characterization, which may cross kinds.
example: Tagged links + 4 fundamental relations: aggregation, exhibition, generalization, instantiation.

definition: Each fundamental relation refines one refineable (whole, exhibitor, general, class) into refinees (parts, features, specializations, instances). Forward names: aggregation, exhibition, generalization, classification; complementary: participation, characterization, specialization, instantiation.
example: Refinee kinds map to folding/unfolding kinds.

definition: Whole aggregates parts. Black solid triangle; apex to whole. Whole and parts share Perseverance. Incomplete sets use an extra bar + 'at least one other part' phrasing.
opl_template: Whole consists of Part1, Part2, ..., and Partn.
example: Resource Description Framework Statement consists of Subject, Predicate, and Object.

definition: Exhibitor exhibits features: attributes (object features) and operations (process features). Only structural relation crossing the object-process boundary. Triangle-in-triangle symbol. Attribute states are its values.
opl_template: Exhibitor exhibits Attribute1, ..., as well as Operation1, ... (object exhibitor: attributes first; process exhibitor: operations first)
example: Vehicle exhibits Travelling Medium. Travelling Medium of Vehicle can be ground, air, and water surface.

definition: General generalizes specializations of the same Perseverance; empty triangle. Specializations inherit four element kinds: parts, features, tagged structural links, procedural links. Multiple inheritance allowed; participants may be overridden via specialization.
opl_template: Specialization is a General. / Spec1, ..., and Specn are General.
example: Car, Aircraft, and Ship are Vehicles.

definition: Inherited attribute whose distinct values identify the specializations; expressed by state-specified characterization link: each specialization exhibits one fixed value. Multiple discriminating attributes -> Cartesian-product lattice.
opl_template: Specialized-object exhibits value-name Attribute-Name. / Attribute of Specialization is value.
example: Travelling Medium of Car is ground.

definition: Class (object class or process class) classifies instances created by giving explicit values to the class pattern. Circle-in-triangle symbol. Instance values must fall within class value ranges. No complete/incomplete distinction. Instantiation does not form hierarchies (book).
opl_template: Instance is an instance of Class.
example: Jack Robinson is an instance of Adult. Gender of Jack Robinson is male.

definition: Modeller-defined static relations between like-kind things: unidirectional (tag), null-tagged ('relates to'), bidirectional (two tags, two sentences), reciprocal (one/no tag, 'are related'). Open arrowhead / harpoon notation.
opl_template: Source tag Destination. | Source relates to Destination. | A and B are reciprocity-tag.
example: Airport serves City. Engine and Gearbox are attached.

definition: Process class = pattern of happening (subprocess sequence) involving object classes in its pre/post sets. Process instance = particular occurrence with object instances and start/end timestamps.
example: Process instance identified by involved object instances + timestamps.

definition: Connect objects (or states) with processes; three families: transforming (consumption, result, effect), enabling (agent, instrument), control (event 'e', condition 'c', invocation, exception). Any incoming/outgoing transforming or enabling link may be state-specified.
example: Transformee and enabler are roles relative to a process.

definition: Process consumes (destroys, eliminates) the consumee. Closed-arrow from object to process. Precondition: consumee exists (wait). Consumption immediate at activation unless rate property + quantity attribute model gradual consumption.
opl_template: Processing consumes Consumee.
example: Welding consumes Steel Part A and Steel Part B.

definition: Consumption from a specified state: process activates only when consumee is at that state (wait), then consumes it.
opl_template: Process consumes specified-state Object.
example: Eating consumes edible Food.

definition: Process creates (generates, yields) the resultee. Closed-arrow from process to object. Generation immediate at completion (or rate-based). Postcondition: resultee exists. No control modifiers on result links.
opl_template: Processing yields Resultee.
example: Welding yields Steel Part AB.

definition: Process yields the resultee at a specified state; postcondition includes the state. If the object has an initial state, attach the result link to the object (not the initial state) to avoid ambiguity.
opl_template: Process yields specified-state Object.
example: Mining yields raw Copper. Machining yields pre-tested Part.

definition: Process affects a stateful object: unspecified state change. Bidirectional double-arrow. Affectee in both pre/post sets; in transition during performance; indeterminate if process aborts (use exception handling).
opl_template: Processing affects Affectee.
example: Editing affects File.

definition: Pair: input link (state s1 -> process) + output link (process -> state s2), same object. Pre: at s1; post: at s2. THE canonical OPM state-transition construct.
opl_template: Process changes Object from s1 to s2.
example: Purifying changes Copper from raw to pure.

definition: Input-specified: 'changes Object from s1.' - output state = default state or per state probability distribution. Output-specified: 'changes Object to s2.' - any input state.
opl_template: Process changes Object from input-state. | Process changes Object to output-state.
example: Testing changes Sample from awaiting test. Cleaning & Painting changes Engine Hood to painted.

definition: Human (or human-group) enabler necessary for activation and throughout performance; not transformed; disappearance immediately ends the process. Black lollipop from agent to process.
opl_template: Agent handles Processing.
example: Welder handles Welding.

definition: Agent must be at the specified state for activation/performance.
opl_template: Specified-state Agent handles Processing.
example: Sober Pilot handles Flying.

definition: Non-human, non-decision-making enabler; required for the process to occur; neither consumed nor changed (wear disregarded; if change matters, model as affectee). White lollipop.
opl_template: Processing requires Instrument.
example: Sintering requires Sintering Oven.

definition: Instrument must be at the specified state; process occurs iff so (wait semantics).
opl_template: Processing requires specified-state Instrument.
example: Moving requires serviced Moving Truck.

definition: Control modifier 'c' on any incoming link adds skip semantics: if source object (at state, if specified) missing, the process is bypassed, not waited for. Kinds: condition consumption/effect/agent/instrument + state-specified variants. No condition on result links.
opl_template: Process occurs if Object exists|is state, in which case ..., otherwise Process is skipped.
example: Precise Measuring occurs if LASER Meter exists, otherwise Precise Measuring is skipped.

definition: ISO-exact realization of 'process occurs only if object is in state s' WITH skip semantics. For wait semantics use the unannotated state-specified link instead.
opl_template: Process occurs if Instrument is specified-state, otherwise Process is skipped.
example: Book legacy: Accessing occurs if Card is valid.

definition: Control modifier 'e': source object creation/appearance/state entry initiates precondition evaluation; event lost afterwards. Kinds: consumption/effect/agent/instrument event + state-specified variants. No result event link.
opl_template: Object initiates Process, which consumes|affects|requires Object. / Agent initiates and handles Process.
example: Food initiates Eating, which consumes Food.

definition: Process-to-process control links: on completion, source immediately initiates destination (or itself). Implies transient object created and immediately consumed. Lightning-bolt notation.
opl_template: Invoking-process invokes invoked-process. | Invoking-process invokes itself.
example: Product Finishing invokes Product Shipping.

definition: Time-exception routing from a process violating Minimal/Maximal Duration to a handling process. One bar = overtime, two bars = undertime.
opl_template: Overtime Handling Process occurs if duration of Source Process exceeds max-duration time-units.
example: Model premature/late endings explicitly for robust control flow.

definition: Multiple same-kind links not sharing a contour point = AND; expressed as ONE OPL sentence with 'and' lists.
opl_template: Safe Opening requires Key A, Key B, and Key C.
example: Meal Preparing yields Starter, Entree, and Dessert.

definition: Same-kind links sharing a common point = link fan with XOR (exactly one) or OR (at least one) semantics; divergent/convergent ends; optional probabilities (12.7).
opl_template: (see ISO clause 12 for fan OPL forms)
example: XOR: exactly one divergent-end thing exists/occurs.

definition: Graphics and text are semantically equivalent expressions of one model; every construct has exactly one OPL sentence; OPL paragraph per OPD.
example: Non-technical stakeholders read OPL; engineers read OPD.

definition: Thing names: bold, every word capitalized. States/values: bold, lowercase (except sentence-initial). Tags: bold. Reserved grammar words: non-bold.
example: Sober Pilot handles Flying. - 'Sober' capitalized sentence-initially only.

definition: ISO 19450 Annex A (normative) specifies the complete OPL grammar in ISO/IEC 14977 EBNF; graph grammar for valid OPDs in Annex E/F.
example: Use Annex A productions for parser construction in later phases.
source_reference: ISO Annex A para 2728+; Annex E para 4582+

definition: Things declare non-default generic properties: 'Thing is physical.' 'Thing is environmental and physical.' Defaults unexpressed once the thing is linked.
opl_template: Thing is [essence][ and affiliation] object|process.
example: Testing is environmental and physical.

definition: Every node is object, process, or state-of-an-object; every edge is a classified link. Unclassifiable elements are errors.
example: A 'state' floating outside any object is invalid.

definition: Check each link kind's legal source/target per the legality matrix: structural like-kind (except exhibition); procedural object/state<->process; invocation/exception process->process.
example: Aggregation between an object and a process: error.

definition: Each graphical construct must produce exactly one OPL sentence matching its template, and vice versa; orphan sentences or undumped links are errors.
example: Link without sentence: incomplete model serialization.

definition: For every effect-link family: states belong to the affectee; s1 != s2 for input-output pairs; input-specified requires default state/distribution; affectee stateful.
example: 'changes Copper from raw to pure' - raw, pure must be Copper states.

definition: Every process: >=1 transforming link; non-empty pre/post sets; role uniqueness per object; no contradicting facts across OPDs.
example: A process with only enablers and no transformee is invalid.

definition: Wait-vs-skip intent, missing fan semantics (AND vs OR/XOR), unnamed tags, incomplete refinee sets without annotation: flag, choose documented default, log assumption. No code generation before validation passes.
example: Default: state requirement = wait semantics; log as assumption.
